body.title: [akien] Daemon on isolated network — capture WU format, write CSV
body.intention: I intend that the daemon captures WU format weather data from the isolated device and persists it to CSV for dashboard consumption.
body.description:
Daemon listens on 10.0.0.230:80 for HTTP requests from AcuRite device. Parse WU (Weather Underground) format: /weatherstation/updateweatherstation?ID=...&tempf=...&humidity=...&windspeedmph=...&rainin=...&baromin=...&dewptf=...&uv_index=... (hub sends two messages per cycle ~20s apart — one temp/humidity, one wind/rain; merge into single CSV row per sensor_id). Write rows to /home/akien/AcuRite/weather.csv with headers: timestamp, sensor_id, sensor_name, temp_f, humidity_pct, wind_mph, wind_gust_mph, wind_dir_deg, rain_in, pressure_inhg, dew_point_f, uv_index. Log all requests to datacenter_logs/acurite_device/daemon.log. Handle device disconnection gracefully (don't crash).

**Affected files:** new file devices/acurite_device/isolated_daemon.py or lab/claudecode/acurite_isolated_daemon.py (TBD per discussion)
**Design rules:** log-state-changes (log every HTTP request + parse), log-interface-crossings (HTTP ↔ CSV), datacenter_logs hierarchy
**Scope boundary:** Daemon only; network config is separate; weather.html integration is separate
**Completion criteria:** (1) Daemon listens on 10.0.0.230:80; (2) Parses WU format requests; (3) Merges two-message-per-cycle pattern correctly (same sensor_id, different fields); (4) Writes to weather.csv with all fields; (5) Logs requests + merges to datacenter_logs/acurite_device/daemon.log; (6) Handles device disconnection without crashing; (7) CSV is readable by weather.html
**Test plan:** (1) Connect device to isolated segment, power on; (2) Monitor daemon logs, verify requests arrive; (3) Check weather.csv for new rows appearing; (4) Verify two messages per cycle merge into one row; (5) Disconnect device, verify daemon stays running; (6) Reconnect, verify data flows again
